title: castilleja parviflora var. douglasii
intro: Castilleja parviflora var. douglasii, also known as Indian paintbrush, is a flowering plant species in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory qualities. The health benefits of Indian paintbrush include its potential to aid in the treatment of sore throats, coughs, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to reduce fever and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, Indian paintbrush is often made into a tea or tincture, and can also be used topically as a salve or poultice to apply its medicinal properties directly to the affected area.
